name: Protein Sugar Cookies (Caramel + Graham)
description: Protein sugar cookie reworked into a salted brown-sugar-caramel + graham cookie, bakery-style: big graham cracker shards pressed onto the TOP before baking so they anchor, toast, and stay crisp (not crumbs folded into the dough, which go soft), then a REAL brown-sugar caramel drizzle (the SF syrup didn't taste good enough) taken to the thin saucy stage, finished with flaky salt and a fresh graham shard set into the caramel. No pan-slap here - it would knock the shards loose. The caramel is the main calorie cost, drizzled at ~half a batch (about 1 tbsp over the 4 cookies); dial it to taste. Computed batch macros ~574 kcal / 41P / 66C / 16F (143/cookie at 4; ratio 14.1).

payload.name: honey graham crackers (broken into big shards)

payload.instructionText: Once the cookies are set and cooled, drizzle with the brown-sugar caramel - cook it only to the thin saucy/pourable stage so it threads off a spoon for drizzling - then add a small pinch of flaky salt over it (salted caramel, the contrast that keeps it from reading one-note sweet) and press one reserved graham shard into the caramel on each for a fresh, just-placed look. Chill briefly to set the drizzle.

payload.text: Break the graham into big shards (rough rectangles/quarters), not crumbs, and press them onto the top of the cookies BEFORE baking so they anchor, toast lightly, and stay crisp - folding crumbs into the dough buries them and they go soft. Reserve a couple of shards to set into the caramel after baking for a fresh, bakery look. The caramel is the real brown-sugar caramel sub-recipe (taken to the thin saucy stage) - genuine caramelized flavor that the SF syrup can't match; it's the main calorie cost in this cookie, so drizzle to taste.

payload.instructionText: Stir to combine, then stop and let the bowl sit untouched 5 minutes so the whey and pudding starches swell into a shapeable dough (if still tacky, chill 5 more minutes). Then cover and chill the dough ~30 minutes — same as the browned-butter cookies: a proper chill firms it for cleaner shaping and a thicker, chewier bake.

Preheat oven/air fryer to 330°F. Divide the chilled dough into 4 equal balls on parchment and press firmly with your palm or a glass to your target thickness — this dough sets in the shape you give it, it won't spread, so keep them on the thicker side for a chewy center rather than thin and dry. Then break the graham crackers into big rough shards (rectangles/quarters, not crumbs) and press 1-2 onto the top of each cookie, pushing them in slightly so they anchor and bake in — surface graham toasts lightly and stays crisp instead of going soft in the dough. Reserve a couple of shards for the finish.

Bake at 330°F. These are smaller and far leaner than the browned-butter cookies, so start checking at ~8-10 minutes to be safe - pull the moment the edges are set and the centers still look slightly underdone. They stay pale (Splenda won't brown), so never wait for golden tops: thicker-pressed cookies trend toward the 10-min end, thin ones can be done by 7-8. (No pan-slap on this cookie - the graham shards are planted on top and a bang would knock them loose.)
